data.title: Allegiant sees June passenger traffic increase 9.2%
data.description:
• Allegiant Travel Company (NASDAQ:ALGT) on Thursday reported a 9.6% increase in scheduled service in June nearly to 2M, driving up the total number of miles flown by paying passengers (revenue passenger miles) by nearly 9.8% to 1.77M.
  • Available seat miles rose more than 12.4% to 2.09M.
  • ALGT +1.99% premarket to $51.68.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HENSOLDT AG ADR reports 1H results
data.description:
• HENSOLDT AG ADR press release (OTCPK:HAGHY): 1H Adjusted EBITDA rises slightly to EUR 107 million
  • Revenue grows to EUR 944 million in the first half of 2025 (previous year: EUR 849 million)

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Mastercard tops Q2 estimates; initiates Q3 and updates FY outlook
Dyadic International prices public offering of 6.05M shares of common stock
data.description:
• Dyadic International (NASDAQ:DYAI) announced on Thursday the pricing of its previously announced underwritten public offering of 6.05M shares of common stock at a public offering price of $0.95 per share. 
  • The company expects the gross proceeds from this offering to be approximately $5.75M, before deducting the underwriting discount and other offering expenses. 
  • Dyadic intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes, such as product development, sales, and marketing.

data.title: Roblox's upbeat bookings outlook drives investor optimism; shares soar
data.description:
Shares of Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) surged on Thursday after the online gaming platform provided a strong bookings forecast and saw robust growth for key operating metrics in the second quarter, which made investors overlook its wider loss and surging costs.

RBLX stock is up 20.5% in premarket trading on the NYSE.

For the full year, bookings, which the company defines as revenue plus the change in deferred revenue and other adjustments, are forecast in the $5.87B to $5.97B range, raised from $5.29B to $5.36B, above the consensus estimate of $5.42B.

For the current quarter, it expects bookings between $1.59B and $1.64B, ahead of the $1.34B estimate.

In Q2, bookings were up 51% at $1.44B and topped the consensus estimate of $1.26B. Average daily active users rose 41% to 111.8M and beat the estimates of 110.95M. Bookings per DAU on average were up 7% to $12.86 (est. $12.09).

Users on Roblox engaged in 27.4B hours (est. 23.14B) of gameplay and activity during the quarter, up 58% year-over-year.

"We are encouraged by the momentum across Roblox as we look to capture 10% of the global gaming content market flowing through our platform," CEO David Baszucki said.

The company's free cash flow surged 58% to $176.7M (est. $154.2M).

Net loss for the three months ended June 30 widened to $278.4M, from a loss of $205.9M for the same period last year, as total expenses surged nearly 24% to $1.40B.

On a per-share basis, the San Mateo, California-based company lost 41 cents, while Wall Street was expecting it to lose 36 cents.

Revenue rose 21% to $1.08B but missed expectations of $1.11B.

Additionally, the product chief of the company, Manuel Bronstein, decided to resign from his position to pursue personal interests and entrepreneurial opportunities.

Bronstein's resignation will be effective on September 30, but he will remain as an advisor through April 13, 2026, to assist with the transition.

data.title: Medical Properties Q2 earnings, revenue top estimates, as rents recover
data.description:
Medical Properties Trust (NYSE:MPW) stock climbed 2.9% in Thursday premarket trading after the REIT posted stronger-than-expected Q2 adjusted results, even as a tenant bankruptcy continued to affect its bottom line.

The hospital and medical facility REIT, also called MPT, posted Q2 normalized FFO per share of $0.14, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.13, unchanged from $0.14 in Q1 and down from $0.23 in last year's Q2.

Including a charge of  $111M, or $0.18 per share, or impairment and fair market value adjustments primarily related to the completed sale of PHP Holdings and certain Prospect Medical Group bankruptcy transactions, MPT's Q2 2025 net loss came to $0.16 per share. 

Q2 total revenue of $240.4M, topping the $231.9M consensus, increased from $223.8M in the prior quarter and fell from $266.6M a year ago.

"During the quarter, our portfolio of new operators continued to successfully ramp operations around the country," said Chairman President and CEO Edward K. Aldag, Jr. "As expected, rental income from these operators increased significantly quarter-over-quarter and, in turn, we remain confident in our visibility to annualized pro rata cash rent of more than $1B by the fourth quarter of 2026."

Cash rental income from new tenants rose to $11.0M from Q1's $3.4M, representing 96% of scheduled collections. Scheduled collections for Q3 approximate $17M.

Q2 expenses of $233.5M vs. $229.3M in Q1 and $246.7M in last year's Q2.

As of June 30, 2025, MPT’s  (NYSE:MPW) portfolio included 392 properties and ~39,000 licensed beds leased to or mortgaged by 53 hospital operating companies across the U.S., as well as in the U.K., Switzerland, Germany, Spain, Finland, Colombia, Italy and Portugal.

Conference call at 11:00 AM ET

data.title: Ontex reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Ontex press release (OTCPK:ONXXF): Q2 Adj. EBITDA of €35.7M
  • Revenue of €429.7M (-5.8% Y/Y)

data.title: Repligen partners with Novasign to use ML in filtration systems
data.description:
• Repligen Corporation (NASDAQ:RGEN) said it has formed a partnership with Novasign to enhance Repligen's filtration systems by incorporating Novasign's machine learning and modeling technology.
  • As part of this collaboration, Repligen will provide investment to support Novasign's growth and operational expansion.

data.title: Cenovus Energy declares CAD 0.20 dividend
data.description:
• Cenovus Energy (NYSE:CVE) declares CAD 0.20/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable Sept. 29; for shareholders of record Sept. 15; ex-div Sept. 15.
  • See CVE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Clariant AG reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Clariant AG (OTCPK:CLZNF): Q2 sales of CHF 968 million were flat in local currencies, as growth in catalysts and adsorbents & additives offset a slight decline in care shemicals.
  • Q2 EBITDA margin before exceptional items increased by 200 basis points to 17.5% from 15.5% in Q2 2024.
  • H1 operating cash flow of CHF 116 million, with free cash flow conversion (LTM) of 37%.
  • Outlook 2025: 17–18% EBITDA margin before exceptional items confirmed, with local currency sales growth lowered to 1 – 3 %; medium-term targets confirmed.
